title: CloudFront OAC, Private S3 Origins, and SSE-KMS · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs cloudfront oac, private s3 origins, and sse-kms from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for private origin authorization contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript S3 Origin Website Boundary · changed case · Review S3 Origin Website…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3692 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates S3 Origin Website Boundary without dropping earlier contracts?

stimulus.title: Oai Oac Migration case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2978 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Replacing the origin identity ID in one policy statement is a zero-risk migration. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback. Artifact under review: private origin authorization contract.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply Oai Oac Migration without its stated preconditions, omit the private origin authorization contract trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Oai Oac Migration for private origin authorization contract.
The record names the assumption, applies Oai Oac Migration, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Oai Oac Migration record says private origin authorization contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Oai Oac Migration.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.
